digest.component_or_area: audit web server scaffolding (rebar audit serve)
propositions: Add a default-false [ui] enabled config flag wired fully through UiConfig so it round-trips via rebar.toml, REBAR_UI_ENABLED, and -c ui.enabled without being reported as an unknown key.
When [ui] enabled is false (default), import rebar pulls in no web dependency and rebar audit serve refuses to start with a message naming the [ui] enabled flag and exits non-zero.
When the ui extra is absent, the lazy import of rebar.audit.server yields an actionable 'install nava-rebar[ui]' message instead of a traceback and exits non-zero.
Add a serve subcommand to the existing audit_cli dispatcher that starts a FastAPI server bound to 127.0.0.1 on default port 8765, overridable with --port.
rebar audit serve --host with a non-loopback address prints an exposure warning to stderr before binding, while the default 127.0.0.1 does not.
The enabled server serves a read-only index page that enumerates and lists tickets having audit data (non-empty plan_reviews, completion, or code_reviews) with links to per-ticket pages.
